rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH RIESER VS. C.C. MOORE & CO. ENGINEERS
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 26, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Hennessy Industries, Inc.¿s demurrer to plaintiff¿s first amended complaint is overruled. The doctrine of judicial estoppel does not bar plaintiff¿s claims. Both parties¿ requests for judicial notice are granted. Based solely on plaintiff¿s complaint and the documents of which this court takes judicial notice, there is no showing that plaintiff has taken two positions or that any positions taken by plaintiff are inconsistent. Further, even if it had been demonstrated that plaintiff had taken two inconsistent positions, there is no showing that plaintiff¿s failure to amend his bankruptcy Schedule B to add his asbestos claims was not a result of ignorance or mistake. On defendant¿s alternate ground for demurrer, plaintiff does have standing to sue. (See Kelsey v. Waste Management of Alameda County (1999) 76 Cal.App.4th 590.) Defendant has 10 days to answer. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES SHIFFER et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 26, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Dillingham Construction, N.A., Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied as untimely. Defendant failed to provide proper notice pursuant to C.C.P. sections 437c(a) and 1010.6 and CRC 2.251. Defendant¿s proof of service shows that defendant electronically served its motion on January 10, 2013. With a noticed hearing date of March 26, 2013, defendant failed to provide proper notice by failing to extend the notice period by two court days as required by C.C.P. 1010.6. This court¿s order granting preference did not shorten such notice period nor did it state that e-service is the equivalent of personal service. Plaintiffs¿ objection based on improper notice is sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
